title: Cleopatra
author: Daniel, Samuel
plot: Antony is dead, but Cleopatra still lives, holed up in her monument. She wants to join her lover in death, but is restrained by fear of how her children will be treated afterwards. Egypt has been conquered by Caesar, who visits the defeated Queen; she asks for mercy and hands over an inventory of her treasure, but her secretary Seleucus betrays her by notifying Caesar that she has kept some items back. Attempting to get her son Caesario out of Egypt, she entrusts him to his tutor Rhodon; but Rhodon is bribed by Caesar’s agents and hands the boy over to be killed by the Romans. Charmed by her beauty, Dolabella warns Cleopatra that Caesar intends to display her in his triumph, so she procures two asps and kills herself with them. ‘ —— ° ; 1593 B-Text In this version, more is told of the death of Antony before the start of the action: how Cleopatra sent him a false report of her death, which precipitated his attempted suicide, and how he was hoisted up into the monument to die. Cleopatra’s wish to kill herself is initially kept secret: she is first seen making arrangements to send Caesario to India, intending that he should one day return to supplant Caesar as the ruler of the Roman Empire.

title: Richard the Third
author: Shakespeare, William
plot: The House of York has won the civil war, but the deformed RGGHaFaNGfNGIOucester considers himself unsuited to such peacetime pursuits as love; instead he will be a villain. He engineers his brother @aeHiGe’s imprisonment over a prophecy which suggests that Clarence poses a threat to the sickly King Edward’s issue. BAGYSAINe meets Richard as she is taking King Henry's body for burial; the corpse’s wounds bleed afresh, identifying Richard as the murderer, but he still succeeds in wooing her. Rancorous rivalry between Richard and the Queen’s recently ennobled family is temporarily suspended when , Henry VI’s widow, curses the lot of them with misery and early death. Richard sends murderers to dispose of Clarence quickly, before the King can die; Clarence dreams of drowning, and, despite the conscience of one of the murderers, he is stabbed and drowned in a butt of wine. The King tries to reconcile his factious courtiers, and learns that his order for Clarence’s death has been carried out: the countermand arrived too late. He dies, and Richard has the P#ifiée brought to London to be crowned. Several members of the Queen’s family are imprisoned; the Queen herself takes sanctuary with her younger son. : The Prince arrives in London and has his brother brought from sanctuary to join him; they are lodged together in the Tower. Richard sounds out Hastings and other barons to see if they will support his claim to the throne, and promises Bulékingham the earldom of Hereford in reward for his help. Hastings disregards a warning against Richard, declares his loyalty to the Prince, and attends a council to discuss the coronation. Richard and Buckingham claim that Richard has been deformed by witchcraft, and that Hastings is complicit; he is arrested and beheaded. Richard has Buckingham suggest to the citizens of London that Richard is the lawful heir to the throne because the Prince was the issue of a bigamous marriage, and so_ illegitimate. Richard feigns unwillingness to accept the crown, but capitulates under pressure. After his coronation, he tries to persuade Buckingham to arrange the murder of his nephews in the Tower. When Buckingham hesitates, he makes alternative arrangements and refuses him the promised earldom; this spells the end of their alliance. Both the princes and Queen Anne are murdered; Richard seeks to secure his position by marrying his niece, King Edward’s daughter, using her mother as a proxy wooer. As characters fall and die, Queen Margaret observes the outcome of her curse. 1593 Barons flee to the BAMMOPFERIEHMOnd, who plans to join with Buckingham’s forces in an attack on Richard; but this is pre-empted by the capture and execution of Buckingham. Richard takes Lord Stanley's son hostage to guarantee the father’s loyalty. The rival armies meet at Bosworth. On the night before the battle, the leaders are visited by the ghosts of Richard’s victims, who curse Richard and wish success to Richmond. Stanley refuses to support Richard, but his son’s execution is deferred until after the battle. Richard loses his horse in the battle: his forces are routed and he is killed by Richmond. Stanley crowns the victor.

title: The Reign of King Edward the Third
author: AnonymousShakespeare, William
plot: KingledwWardlll of England hears of his claim to the French throne by his mother’s right, and plans an invasion. The French have induced the Scots to invade England, and they have laid siege to the Countess of 1593 Salisbury’s castle. King Edward goes north to deal with them, and they flee at his approach, but the Countess persuades him to stay rather than pursue them. He falls in love with her, but neither his wooing nor her father’s unwilling advocacy can overcome her fidelity to her husband. She breaks his obsession by first demanding the deaths of his queen and her husband, then threatening to stab herself. With his troops mustered, Edward sets off for France. The French gather their allies about them, but their navy fails to prevent the English from landing. Edward’s army lays waste the country, and the inhabitants flee. PRGSIBAWArd is formally armed, and his father refuses to commit troops to help him out of danger at the battle of Crécy; the honour of his victory is thereby the greater. MHeEArNonSalisbiity releases his prisoner Willli@rs to seek safe conduct for him to Calais, on condition that he returns a prisoner if unsuccessful. His insistence on keeping his oath to return persuades the reluctant Dalipiin to issue a passport. Emboldened by an ambiguous prophecy, the French give battle to Prince Edward. Salisbury is captured, but produces his passport; Kimggohih is reluctantly compelled to honour it. The terms of the prophecy are fulfilled: the French army is frightened by ravens and, out of ammunition, the Prince’s army fights with the stones of the earth. The French are routed and King John taken prisoner. King Edward besieges Calais: after expelling its poor men to save food, the town surrenders; but Edward’s terms are that six leading burgesses must give themselves up within two days to suffer what fate he pleases. He is about to attack when they do so; and QHeeHEPHTippa persuades Edward to spare their lives. THEIKINGOHSESts is taken prisoner, and his captor brings him to France to surrender him to King Edward in person. Prince Edward also brings his father the gift of a captive king; King John is taken prisoner to England.

title: The Taming of a Shrew
author: Anonymous
plot: Forcibly ejected from an alehouse, Sly is sleeping off his drunkenness when he is found by a lord, who brings him home and tricks him into thinking himself a nobleman. A play is performed for him: The Taming of a Shrew. In the play, Aurelius of Sestos, newly arrived in Athens, falls in love with Philema; his friend Polidor already loves her sister, Emelia. To pursue his love, Aurelius pretends to be a wealthy merchant’s son and has his man Valeria assume his aristocratic identity. However, the girls’ father Alfonso has decreed that his eldest daughter Kate, a shrewish scold, must be married before them. The two wooers decide to persuade Ferando to marry Kate, but Alfonso has already promised him six thousand crowns to undertake the same task He and Kate meet unchaperoned, and they wrangle, but the marriage is arranged for the following Sunday; Kate seems outwardly unwilling but consents in an aside. Ferando goes to prepare his country house. Valeria is sent to teach Kate the lute, but she behaves aggressively. On the wedding day Ferando arrives in base clothes and forces Kate to leave with him immediately after the ceremony. When they arrive in the country, he claims that the dinner is burnt, and beats his servants. She is kept hungry, and denied her choice of clothing. Aurelius has Philotus pose as his father to persuade Alfonso that he is wealthy enough to marry Philema. He and Polidor are married to Kate’s younger sisters. Kate and Ferando visit them, after Kate submits to Ferando’s will. On the way, they meet the Duke of Sestos (who has come to Athens in disguise to spy on his son), and mock him. The Duke exposes Aurelio’s pretence and proposes to have those involved sent to prison, but Sly intervenes in the performance to prevent this. The three new husbands bet on whose wife will come when called. Ferando wins, and Kate explains to her sisters their duty as wives. Sly falls asleep during the performance. The lord puts him back where he was found. He returns home intending to tame his own wife.

title: A Knack to Know an Honest Man
author: Anonymous
plot: Sempronio has attempted to commit adultery with Lelio’s wife, so Lelio fights and wounds him in a duel. The affray is witnessed by three shepherds, who pursue him as a murderer, leaving Sempronio dying. He is found by an old hermit, Philip, who attempts to heal his wounds. His uncle, Servio, appeals to the Duke of Venice for justice against Lelio - though, glad to inherit Sempronio’s estate, he is motivated more by malice than moral outrage. The Duke pronounces Lelio guilty of murder and sentences him to death in his absence; his estate is confiscated, but his wife and daughter are allowed to keep his house. The Duke m ===== PAGE 250 ===== further puts a price on his head and orders that nobody should succour him on pain of banishment. Lelio’s father-in-law Brishio helps him to escape from Venice, but Servio sees him being winched into the ship. Brishio is arrested and banished, and his sons, Zepherius and Orphinio, are left destitute. Philip revives Sempronio, whose near-death experience has made him chaste and ascetic. Philip disguises him as an old man, and he swears not to reveal his true identity until the hermit permits it. He assumes the alias Penitent Experience, and meets Fortunio and Marchetto, who are discussing their interest in Lelio’s family and estate: Fortunio wants the love of Lelio’s daughter, Lucida, but Marchetto is more interested in the money. Sempronio tells them that both gold and beauty are evanescent, and offers to show them how to recognize an honest man; Fortunio accepts him into his service. Marchetto seizes Lelio’s property, and offers to give some of it back to Annetta if she will grant him sexual favours; Fortunio also propositions Lucida. Refused, the men plan to return that night and rape them. Sempronio notifies Zepherius and Orphinio, who intervene and wound Fortunio as he is trying to force an entry. They are imprisoned and Servio tries to have them executed for. murder; but his daughter, Phillida, is in love with Orphinio and releases them, on condition that he will marry her should he ever return to Venice. Servio is arrested and sentenced to death for letting the brothers escape; but he is pardoned after Fortunio recovers, newly penitent, and admits that the brothers were right to stop the attempted rape. Lelio and Brishio flee to Florence and Milan ‘ respectively; but the two dukes are on the point of war over a dowry dispute, and the two refugees are appointed their champions. When the two kinsmen recognize one another, they are unable to fight, and their example inspires the dukes to come to terms and make peace. Orphinio and Zepherius meet Lelio and insist that he return to Venice in the expectation that, if he gives himself up to execution, their father will be recalled. Brishio disavows the plan, but Lelio decides to go anyway, and offers himself to Annetta and Lucida; they refuse to give him up for the reward. Servio arrests him, keen to claim the money for himself; but agrees to hold him prisoner for three days before bringing him before the Duke. When the trial proceeds, Lelio is sentenced to death; but Servio’s delay costs him the reward and earns him a year’s imprisonment. Lelio’s kinsmen all offer their lives in his place, but the Duke is adamant: Brishio, Zepherius, and Orphinio are all pardoned, but Lelio still must die. In the nick of time, ° Philip arrives and identifies Sempronio: no murder has been committed, so there can be no execution. Lelio agrees to marry Lucida to Sempronio. His sentence remitted, Servio is horrified to learn that his nephew is alive, and that he must return the estate.

title: The Two Gentlemen of Verona
author: Shakespeare, William
plot: Walefifihe leaves Verona to broaden his horizons. Protelis stays at home and woos Jiilia) but no sooner is his suit successful than his father decides to send him after Valentine to court. He and Julia exchange rings. In Milan, Proteus finds that Valentine has fallen in love with Sil¥ia and plans to elope with her, using a rope ladder to reach the tower in which her father locks her up at night. Proteus falls for Silvia himself and, knowing that her father prefers Valentine’s rival Hiro, betrays his friend’s confidence to the Duke, who duly banishes Valentine. This only succeeds in ‘alienating Silvia from Thurio and her father, so Proteus is asked to woo her on Thurio’s behalf. Valentine is ambushed by banished Saws, who make him their chief. Leaving her property in the care of her waiting- woman Hwee’, Julia follows Proteus to Milan disguised as a page-boy; there she learns of Proteus’ interest in Silvia, and witnesses one of his wooing attempts. Proteus sends Silvia a gift of a dog, but Bane loses it and offers her his own dog, which behaves badly and relieves itself under the table; Lance takes the blame for the latter offence. Proteus employs the disguised Julia as a go-between, sending her ring as a gift to Silvia, who will not accept it. With Bglami6ii’s help, Silvia absconds to join Valentine; the Duke rides after her with Thurio; Proteus follows in search of Silvia, and the disguised Julia follows him. All except Eglamour are captured by the outlaws. When Silvia rejects Proteus’ advances, he threatens to rape her. Valentine sees him for what he is, but is won over by his repentance, and offers to give up Silvia. Julia faints and mixes up the two rings in her possession; thus her identity is revealed. Threatened by Valentine, Thurio gives up his suit to Silvia; the Duke accepts Valentine as a son-in-law and agrees to recall the outlaws from banishment.

title: Romeo and Juliet
author: Shakespeare, William
plot: After a street fight breaks out between servants of the rival Veronese households of Capulet and Montague, the Prince decrees death for any further breach of the peace in the matter. Romeo, the Montague son, is hopelessly in love with a Capulet lady, Rosaline, and discovers by chance that she is to be present at Old Capulet’s feast that night. He and his friends gatecrash _ the party disguised as masquers; Tybalt notices Romeo and wants to throw him out, but Old Capulet discourages this. Also present is Capulet’s daughter, Juliet, who is intended for marriage to Count Paris. Romeo meets her and they fall in love before knowing one another's identity as members of the two enemy houses. Afterwards, Romeo climbs into the Capulet 1595 orchard, overhears Juliet’s private acknowledgement of her love, declares himself, and makes arrangements for a follow-up contact. Seeing an opportunity to end the feud, Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers. Juliet rendezvouses with Romeo at the Friar’s cell under cover of going to confession, and they become husband and wife. After sending Romeo a challenge, Tybalt attempts to fight him in the street; when Romeo refuses, Mercutio steps in and is killed, whereupon the enraged Romeo avenges his friend by killing Tybalt. The Prince banishes him for the murder. Friar Laurence and the Nurse arrange for the lovers to have a night together before Romeo leaves the city. Thinking Juliet is distressed by Tybalt’s death, her father arranges to hasten her marriage to Paris, and threatens to disown her when she refuses to comply. The Nurse advises her to accept the situation and marry Paris, but Friar Laurence gives her a sleeping potion to take the night before the wedding: it will make her appear dead, and meanwhile he will send to Romeo to fetch her from the family tomb after her funeral. When she returns home apparently ready to submit to the marriage, her father brings the date forward. Juliet takes the potion and is found, apparently dead, on the morning of the planned wedding. The Friar’s letter miscarries, so Romeo hears only of Juliet’s death; he buys poison from a needy apothecary and returns to Verona. Hearing that his arrangements have gone awry, the Friar plans to rescue Juliet from the tomb and keep her hidden until Romeo can fetch her; but Romeo gets there first. He encounters and kills Paris as he tries to break into the tomb, and takes the poison beside Juliet’s body. Meanwhile Paris’s page summons the watch. The Friar arrives to find Romeo dead and Juliet waking; he proposes to take her to a nunnery, but she will not go with him. He flees for fear of being caught by the watch, and Juliet stabs herself. The fathers’ realization of what has happened ends the feud, and each promises to erect a golden statue in memory of other’s dead child.

Balthasar is narned Peter in one stage direction; Gibbons takes
this as an indication that the two roles were designed to be
doubled, but & is more likely to have been an inadvertent

character in Brooke is called Peter.
